name: manage_tickets_tool
summary: Read and mutate the ticket queue from one tool: union of `read_tickets_tool` and `write_tickets_tool`.
Use `get` / `list` / `search` to read, and `create` / `edit` to write.
Finishing a ticket is done with `finish_ticket`, not this tool.
constraints: One `action` per call. Five actions total — see the `action` parameter.
Every action that takes a `key` defaults to the agent's current ticket when `key` is omitted.
`create` stamps `reporter` from the calling agent automatically.
`list` and `search` cap at 50 tickets per call.
anti_patterns: When the agent only needs reads — register `read_tickets_tool` instead. Smaller surface = fewer mistakes.
When the agent should never read tickets it does not own — register `write_tickets_tool` to block listing/searching.
To finish the agent's current ticket — call `finish_ticket`.
To find code or files in the working directory — use `grep_tool` / `glob_tool`.
cautions: IMPORTANT: this tool cannot transition status. Use `finish_ticket` to finish; `Failed` is reserved for ticket-system-driven outcomes (schema-retry trip, policy violations).
ALWAYS finish the current ticket with `finish_ticket` before the response ends. Returning text without a result leaves the ticket `InProgress` and the loop will re-pick it.
output: Reads (`get`): markdown ticket block with key, status, reporter, labels, task body, result.
Reads (`list` / `search`): bullet lines `- KEY [STATUS] [labels] — task preview`. `(no matching tickets)` when empty.
Writes: plain text confirmation, or an error describing the failed action.

key.description: Ticket key (e.g. `TICKET-3`). Used by `get`, `edit`. Defaults to the agent's current ticket. Ignored by `create`, `list`, `search`.

status.description: For `list`: filter by status. One of `Todo`, `InProgress`, `Finished`, `Failed`.

label.description: For `list`: filter to tickets carrying this label (case-sensitive).

query.description: For `search`: case-insensitive substring matched against the task body.

task.description: For `create` (required) or `edit` (optional): the task body — any JSON value (string, object, array, scalar).

labels.description: For `create` or `edit` (optional): label scope. Determines which agents pick up the ticket. Including an agent's name as a label pins the ticket to that agent.

schema.description: For `create` or `edit` (optional): a JSON Schema document. When set, the agent's final result (written via `finish_ticket`) must validate against it; failures count toward `max_schema_retries`.
